problem_setting.name: Open-World Semi-Supervised Learning (Open-World SSL)
problem_setting.description: A generalization of semi-supervised learning and novel class discovery

data_requirements.unlabeled_dataset: Contains instances from both seen and unseen classes

problem_setting.model_task: Either classify instances into previously seen classes or discover new classes and assign instances to them

key_assumptions: New classes can emerge in test data that have never been seen during training
The assumption that training and test data come from the same set of predefined classes rarely holds in real-world scenarios

applications.description: Classifying known cell types and discovering novel cell types in new tissue
Classifying users into predefined interest groups while discovering new interests

robust_semi_supervised_learning.difference: Robust SSL only requires recognizing instances from novel classes as out-of-distribution, while open-world SSL aims to discover individual novel classes and assign instances to them

novel_class_discovery.difference: Novel class discovery assumes unlabeled data is composed only of novel classes, while open-world SSL is more general as instances in unlabeled data can come from both seen and novel classes

limitations_of_existing_approaches: Multi-step approaches combining robust SSL and novel class discovery don't perform well in practice
Treating all classes as 'novel' and then matching them back to seen classes also doesn't work well

figure_references.description: Illustrates the open-world SSL setting, showing that the unlabeled dataset may contain classes not encountered in the labeled set

title: Open-World Semi-Supervised Learning
